headline: Altruist Adds Real-Time Custodial Data to AI Platform Hazel, a First for Wealth Management
summary: Altruist has integrated real-time custodial data — including account, beneficiary, household, holdings, and balance information — into Hazel, its AI platform for independent RIAs, marking what it claims is an industry first. Hazel can now perform household-level concentration analysis, surface asset-class or sector exposure, and run beneficiary summaries in seconds using live custodial records. The platform also unifies CRM, email, and meeting notes alongside this custodial data. Altruist CEO Jason Wenk stated plans to extend Hazel with additional custodian integrations and the ability to initiate actions directly on Altruist from within Hazel. Hazel is available as a standalone platform with a free trial and is designed to serve advisors regardless of where they custody assets.

verified_claims: Altruist integrated real-time custodial data into Hazel, its AI platform for independent RIAs
The custodial data includes account, beneficiary, household, holdings, and balance information
Altruist claims this is an industry first
Hazel can perform household-level concentration analysis
Hazel can surface asset-class or sector exposure
Hazel can run beneficiary summaries in seconds using live custodial records
The platform unifies CRM, email, and meeting notes alongside custodial data
Jason Wenk is the CEO (and Founder) of Altruist
Wenk stated plans to extend Hazel with additional custodian integrations
Wenk stated plans to add the ability to initiate actions directly on Altruist from within Hazel
Hazel is available as a standalone platform with a free trial
Hazel is designed to serve advisors regardless of where they custody assets
unverified_claims: Hazel launched in September 2025 — the source does not mention a September 2025 launch date
Hazel is used by more than 1,000 wealth managers — this statistic does not appear anywhere in the source article
fabricated_claims: 1,000+ wealth managers using Hazel since its September 2025 launch — this specific statistic and the September 2025 launch date are not present in the source and appear to be fabricated or sourced from outside the provided article
_governance.notes: The generated entry introduces two specific claims not found in the source: (1) that Hazel launched in 'September 2025' and (2) that it is used by 'more than 1,000 wealth managers.' The source article does not mention a launch date for Hazel or any user count statistic. These appear fabricated or drawn from an external source not provided. The '1,000+' figure in the Key Stat is especially concerning as a specific number with no grounding in the source. All other claims are well-supported by the source. Verdict is FAIL due to at least one specific statistic that cannot be verified and appears fabricated.
